title: AI RSS Digest — Dify, benchmarked against n8n
problem: Teams drown in RSS/news feeds and waste time scanning for what matters. Worse, when building such an automation, it's unclear which platform to use — a no-code flow tool like n8n, or an LLM-native platform like Dify. Picking wrong means rework.
solution: Built the same 'RSS → AI digest → Slack' pipeline twice. The Dify (self-hosted, Docker) version fetches the feed via a Python Code node through an SSRF proxy, classifies and summarizes up to 5 articles with Claude Haiku into one-line, category-tagged output, and posts to Slack. Hardened with per-attempt timeouts + retry to absorb sandbox/proxy hiccups. Then implemented the n8n equivalent and documented the trade-offs.
result: Automated, category-tagged Slack digests with zero manual triage. The real deliverable is a clear selection guide — 'plumbing → n8n, AI enrichment → Dify' — so clients get the right tool for their requirement, not just a build. Demonstrates tool-neutral judgment across both platforms.

images.caption: Dify workflow: RSS fetch (Code node) → Claude Haiku classification → Slack notification
Real-time Slack digest posted by the Dify workflow
n8n workflow (comparison build): Schedule → Read RSS Feed → Code (JS) → Send to Slack
Daily RSS digest posted by the n8n workflow
